user_prompt: I think you're scoring too many of these as Tier 1. Tighten the criteria — only true high-match.
actual_output:
Understood. I’ll tighten Tier 1 to only jobs with a strong probability of interview conversion, not just decent keyword overlap.

### Revised Tier 1 Criteria — True High-Match Only

A role qualifies as **Tier 1** only if it meets **all or nearly all** of the following:

| Criterion | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Role alignment | Same target title/function, not adjacent |
| Seniority match | Within expected level band — no underleveled or overleveled roles |
| Core stack match | 75%+ overlap with your strongest hands-on skills |
| Domain relevance | Clear overlap with your prior industry/product experience |
| Location/work model | Fully matches your preference without ambiguity |
| Compensation | Meets or exceeds minimum salary with evidence from posting or reliable source |
| Experience requirement | Matches your years of experience, not a stretch |
| Application quality | No major blockers like mandatory assessments, niche credentials, or unrelated requirements |

### New Tier Definitions

#### Tier 1 — High-Probability Interview Targets
Only jobs where your background maps directly to the role.

**Action:**  
Full resume customization, company-specific cover letter, targeted LinkedIn follow-up, and careful application review.

#### Tier 2 — Solid Match
Good fit, but missing one meaningful factor such as domain experience, exact toolset, or ideal seniority alignment.

**Action:**  
Keyword-optimized resume and tailored but lighter cover letter.

#### Tier 3 — Opportunistic
Viable but not highly aligned. Worth applying only if volume goal requires it.

**Action:**  
Minimal customization, no deep company research unless requested.

### Updated Scoring Thresholds

| Tier | Old Threshold | New Threshold |
|---|---:|---:|
| Tier 1 | 80%+ match | 90%+ match |
| Tier 2 | 65–79% | 75–89% |
| Tier 3 | 50–64% | 60–74% |
| Reject | Below 50% | Below 60% or any hard blocker |

### Immediate Adjustment

I’ll rescore the batch and demote any Tier 1 roles that have:

- Vague or missing salary data
- Adjacent but not exact role fit
- Heavy requirements outside your core experience
- Unclear seniority level
- Weak company/domain alignment
- Mandatory assessments or unusual application friction
- “Nice to have” skills that are actually central to the role

### Expected Result

If the previous Tier 1 list had 18 jobs, I’d expect the tightened pass to reduce it to roughly **6–10 true Tier 1 targets**.

Those will get the highest-effort treatment. Everything else moves to Tier 2 or gets rejected if the match is too thin.

I’ll proceed using the stricter Tier 1 filter going forward.
judge_reasoning: The chatbot fully complied with the user's request by defining stricter criteria for Tier 1 applications and explaining how it would adjust its strategy.
